inline_comments.message: That page is giving a raw output of a deployment, that page is now saying that are the mapping to expect, for every port, as they are a few ports that are not assigned by us.

not assigning a node port will result in k8s assigning one of its own. Do we need a deterministic NodePort for this backend port? As far as I know, we don't care much about this port.

If we do care, then the format of the port should be {{.Value.nodePortPrefix }}03 to remain consistent with the NodePort nameming convention.

I agree with you that we may not care about this port.
BUT, as some are deterministic, we cannot have one (or several) that is not as k8s may decide to use as value for this not deterministic port one of the value we have statically written (that's how I've spotted it as it broke my deployment)

for the value,  {{.Value.nodePortPrefix }}03 is already used as  {{.Value.nodePortPrefix }}00  {{.Value.nodePortPrefix }}01  {{.Value.nodePortPrefix }}02  {{.Value.nodePortPrefix }}04  {{.Value.nodePortPrefix }}05  {{.Value.nodePortPrefix }}06 up to around 30...

Ok, I understand. Please try to assign the next available port in the  {{.Value.nodePortPrefix }}XX range.
Thanks
